mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly boosting the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in consumer credit balances indicates a supply-demand mismatch where inventory drawdowns outpace spending, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 103.0 while RSAFS exceeds 490000.0
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 3.0% YoY, aggressive federal funds rate hikes force equity risk premiums higher, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when both CPI and FEDFUNDS rise simultaneously, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 45% in high-inflation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding aggregate demand, which improves corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with quarterly GDP growth rates, or the rolling 3-year Spearman correlation falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll increase exceeding 200,000 jobs signals robust labor demand that sustains consumer spending power, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where payroll growth exceeds the threshold, indicating decoupling between employment and equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts decline while industrial production remains elevated, it reflects a sectoral rotation away from rate-sensitive construction toward manufacturing, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as capital reallocates to higher-margin sectors.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1150.0 while INDPRO stays above 104.0
failure_condition: The joint housing-industrial signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of test windows, or the cross-sectional momentum of the signal decays within 5 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid credit expansion where consumer credit balances rise sharply alongside falling interest rates, liquidity-driven equity valuations expand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by > 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative or statistically insignificant when credit growth and rate cuts coincide, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during high-volatility regimes (VIX > 25).
